chapter_summary: The introduction to the glossary argues that the American educational 'Thoughtworld' maintains dominance through a uniform system of rhetoric and slogans that lack scientific substance. These terms are categorized into five progressive themes designed to prioritize skills over knowledge and natural development over structured instruction.

claims.text: The phrases and slogans used by the American educational community pretend to more soundness, humaneness, substance, and scientific authority than they actually possess.
claims.source_passage: These terms and phrases pretend to more soundness, humaneness, substance, and scientific authority than they in fact possess.

claims.text: American schools of education function to promulgate a uniform system of rhetoric and doctrine to all prospective teachers and professors.
claims.source_passage: Promulgating this system of rhetoric has been an ongoing function of American schools of education, whose uniformity of language and doctrine ensures that every captive of the teacher-certification process and every professor trained to continue the tradition is imbued with educationally correct phrases.

claims.text: Consensus-through-rhetoric is one of the primary instruments used by the Thoughtworld to maintain intellectual dominance.
claims.source_passage: Consensus-through-rhetoric has been one of the main instruments of the Thoughtworld’s intellectual dominance.

claims.text: A doctrinal consensus regarding pedagogical principles exists among the major American educational organizations.
claims.source_passage: The authors claimed that a doctrinal consensus exists among the important educational organizations—including the National Council of Teachers of Mathematics, the Center for the Study of Reading, the National Writing Project, the National Council for the Social Studies, the American Association for the Advancement of Science, the National Council of Teachers of English, the National Association for the Education of Young Children, and the International Reading Association—regarding the best principles of pedagogy.

claims.text: Teachers are professionally instructed to deplore practices like whole-class instruction, textbooks, and standardized tests based on the authority of an ideological consensus.
claims.source_passage: On the authority of this professional consensus, teachers were instructed to de-emphasize and deplore practices represented by bad words like “whole-class instruction,” “passive listening,” “textbooks,” “broad coverage,” “rote memorization of facts,” “competition,” “grades,” and “standardized tests”

claims.text: The pedagogical advice promoted by the educational consensus—including hands-on learning and student responsibility—is not sound.
claims.source_passage: None of this advice is sound.

claims.text: The repetition of educational phrases creates a self-evident quality that induces belief regardless of the phrases' actual truth.
claims.source_passage: Repetition and consensus give the phrases a self-evident, not-to-be-questioned quality which induces those who repeat them to believe them earnestly and implicitly.

claims.text: Almost all familiar American educational phrases are rooted in five themes of progressive education established in the 1910s and 1920s.
claims.source_passage: Almost all the familiar phrases can be grouped under five themes of progressive education, indicating once again the persistence and power of the progressivist doctrines promulgated from Teachers College in the teens and twenties and then replicated in every education school in the nation.

claims.text: Educational doctrines undergo historical transformations where discredited phrases are reborn as new terms to preserve the same underlying principles.
claims.source_passage: When a phrase like “learning by doing” becomes discredited, the principle may still live on in a protean transformation like “hands-on learning.” If the “open classroom” becomes a source of disillusion, it may be reborn as the “multiaged” classroom.

claims.text: False educational doctrines must be exposed to common sense before new educational ideas can be successfully cultivated.
claims.source_passage: Before we Americans can cultivate new educational ideas, the ancient plot of ground must be weeded.

claims.text: Discredited educational principles often survive by undergoing protean transformations into new slogans.
claims.source_passage: Even when a specific educational slogan has become discredited, the principle may still live on in a protean transformation like “hands-on learning.”

claims.text: The prioritization of accessing skills is based on the false premise that modern knowledge changes too rapidly to justify learning specific facts.
claims.source_passage: Accessing skills are currently emphasized by our schools on the grounds that today’s knowledge is changing so rapidly that it will be irrelevant tomorrow. It is better to learn how to “access information”... than to learn a lot of soon-to-be-outmoded facts.

claims.text: Skills such as using a dictionary or encyclopedia are not inherently difficult and do not require extensive instructional time.
claims.source_passage: The dominance of this tool idea... has led our schools to spend a lot of time teaching such techniques as dictionary- or encyclopedia-accessing skills, which must indeed be taught to children but are not inherently difficult skills that take a long time to acquire.

claims.text: Accessing skills cannot replace a student's ready knowledge of subject matters and word meanings.
claims.source_passage: They cannot replace students’ ready knowledge of varied subject matters and word meanings.

claims.text: Students cannot understand the information they look up without prior background knowledge.
claims.source_passage: Even when using an encyclopedia or CD-ROM, students without prior background knowledge cannot understand the things they look up.

claims.text: The ability to learn new things requires a combination of general tactics and domain-specific knowledge.
claims.source_passage: The skill to learn new things consists of both general tactics like accessing skills and a generous amount of “domain-specific” knowledge.

claims.text: There is no reliable evidence to support the effectiveness of self-pacing in education.
claims.source_passage: Going at one’s own pace would seem to be more natural than going at someone else’s, but there is no reliable evidence to support the idea of self-pacing.

claims.text: Externally set timelines, goals, and rewards enhance student achievement more than self-pacing.
claims.source_passage: On the contrary, the data show that the imposition of externally set timelines, goals, and rewards greatly enhances achievement.

claims.text: The doctrine of 'natural' pace can be used to justify leaving disadvantaged children behind.
claims.source_passage: If an inherently able child is slow because of academic and social disadvantages, is it reasonable to say that his or her “natural” pace is slow? Should schools allow such children to fall further behind, or should compensatory efforts be exerted to bring them up to grade level?

claims.text: Nearly all children can be brought to grade level in reading if sufficient effort is applied.
claims.source_passage: Yet reading specialists have concluded that nearly all children can be brought to grade level in reading, though greater effort must be put forth for children who are slower.

claims.text: Multiaged grouping is an experimental practice that lacks empirical support and may be unfair.
claims.source_passage: The doctrine of “natural” pace has achieved its most alarming expression in the practice of multiaged grouping, an experimental practice for which there is little empirical support, and much evidence for its unfairness.

claims.text: The most effective educational systems in the world attempt to bring all children up to grade level without holding back the fastest students.
claims.source_passage: In the early grades, when no one is in a position to pronounce definitively on a child’s “natural” pace, the most effective educational systems in the world try to bring all children up to grade level without holding back the fastest students.

claims.text: The progressive tradition rejects tests that probe isolated knowledge and skills in favor of realistic projects.
claims.source_passage: The progressive tradition has long advocated teaching and testing through “realistic” projects instead of through separate subject matters, and has long rejected tests that probe isolated knowledge and skills.

claims.text: Realistic performance assessments are useful as classroom monitoring and teaching devices, such as in writing courses.
claims.source_passage: These claims are often plausible, particularly when performance tests are used as teaching and monitoring devices in the classroom context; for instance, in a course on writing, it is clearly preferable to use writing tasks as tests rather than to use multiple-choice tests.

claims.text: Performance tests are ineradicably subjective and arbitrary in grading.
claims.source_passage: However, performance tests are only one of many monitoring devices in classroom teaching, and they have been shown to be ineradicably subjective and arbitrary in grading.

claims.text: Performance tests are not appropriate for large-scale, high-stakes testing because they cannot be made fair and accurate at a reasonable cost.
claims.source_passage: They are not appropriate for large-scale, high-stakes testing because no one has been able, even in theory, to make such tests fair and accurate at reasonable cost in money and time.

claims.text: Objective multiple-choice tests are the fairest and most accurate achievement tests currently available.
claims.source_passage: The consensus among psychometricians is that these objective tests, rather than performance tests, are the fairest and most accurate achievement tests available.

claims.text: The ideological attack on the 'banking theory' of schooling (transmission of knowledge) has failed empirically and ideologically.
claims.source_passage: In short, the attack on the banking theory has failed on its own terms both empirically and ideologically.

claims.text: Knowledge functions like 'intellectual capital' in that it enables the accumulation of more knowledge.
claims.source_passage: Under this theory, knowledge functions like money capital in that it enables the accumulation of still more capital—an idea consistent with findings in cognitive psychology.

claims.text: The alternative to the banking theory—focusing on critical-thinking skills—has failed to improve the condition of disadvantaged students.
claims.source_passage: historically the alternative theory has failed to improve the condition of disadvantaged students.

claims.text: The rhetoric of 'break-the-mold' schooling implies that children should be subjected to large-scale novel experiments.
claims.source_passage: The rhetoric of breaking the mold implies that novel educational experiments should be tried on children on a large scale.

claims.text: Many 'break-the-mold' changes are simply failed progressive methods enhanced with technology.
claims.source_passage: Other proposed changes, concerning the goals, contents, and methods of education, have turned out to be already-failed versions of progressive methods, which are now to be enhanced with “technology”

claims.text: The progressive claim that subject-centered instruction ignores the individuality and feelings of the child is a mere caricature.
claims.source_passage: The opposition between subject and child implies that focusing on subject matter is equivalent to inhumane and ineffective schooling. This picture is mere caricature.

claims.text: Children are more interested in high-quality subject-matter teaching than they are in affectively oriented, child-centered classrooms.
claims.source_passage: Observation has shown, on the contrary, that children are more interested by good subject-matter teaching than by an affectively oriented, child-centered classroom.

claims.text: The progressive anti-subject-matter position is inherently anti-intellectual.
claims.source_passage: The anti-subject-matter position is essentially anti-intellectual.

claims.text: A classroom that fails to teach children necessary subjects and skills cannot be accurately described as 'child-centered.'
claims.source_passage: Such failure cannot under any principled use of language be described as “child-centered.”

claims.text: Effort and learning decline in educational environments where grades and tests have been abolished.
claims.source_passage: what is even more important educationally, effort and learning have declined wherever grades and tests have been abolished.

claims.text: Human nature includes an inherent residue of competitiveness that cannot be eliminated by educational policy.
claims.source_passage: Human nature has proved to be robust. Evolutionary psychologists have argued that all humans retain a residue of competitiveness.

claims.text: Well-devised tests improve student learning during a course of study.
claims.source_passage: But the use of grades and of well-devised tests during a course of study has been shown to improve learning.

claims.text: It is false that only self-discovered knowledge is reliably understood and remembered by students.
claims.source_passage: it is not the case, as constructivists imply, that only such self-discovered knowledge will be reliably understood and remembered.

claims.text: Being 'told' information (direct instruction) is a constructive, non-passive cognitive process for the listener.
claims.source_passage: But since being told things is also a constructive, nonpassive process, the quasi-scientific claim that constructivism favors discovery learning is completely unfounded.

claims.text: Discovery learning is the least effective method of instruction in a teacher's repertory.
claims.source_passage: Research into teaching methods has consistently shown that discovery learning is the least effective method of instruction in the teacher’s repertory.

claims.text: The term 'constructivism' is used by educationists to give progressive ideology a false sense of scientific authority.
claims.source_passage: “Constructivism” is a good example of the way technical terms are sometimes used to give progressive ideas a spurious scientific-sounding authority.

claims.text: Cooperative learning is not effective when used as the principal or exclusive means of instruction.
claims.source_passage: It has not been effective when used as the principal or exclusive means of instruction.

claims.text: Progressive emphasis on group cooperation can discourage capable children from pursuing higher levels of achievement.
claims.source_passage: Recently, parents have complained that capable children who want to do more and better work are sometimes discouraged on the grounds of “not cooperating” with the group.

claims.text: Cooperative learning can promote the education of both advanced and less advanced students provided their academic preparation is not too far apart.
claims.source_passage: promote the education of both groups, so long as the two groups are not too far apart in academic preparation.

claims.text: The method of cooperative learning retains vestiges of its historical origins in progressivist practices where group cooperation was elevated above individual achievement.
claims.source_passage: The method still retains vestiges of its historical origins in progressivist practices, when group cooperation was elevated above competition and individual achievement.

claims.text: The orchestration of multiple groups within a classroom is difficult and requires careful monitoring, clear purposes, and definite incentives.
claims.source_passage: The wise and effective orchestration of several groups in a classroom is difficult to do well, needing careful monitoring, clear purposes, and definite incentives.

claims.text: Cooperative learning is an excellent method of instruction only when used in conjunction with whole-class instruction.
claims.source_passage: Cooperative learning, used with restraint, can be an excellent method of instruction when used in conjunction with whole-class instruction.

claims.text: In the progressive tradition, 'critical thinking' is incorrectly used as a counterpoise to the teaching of factual knowledge.
claims.source_passage: In the progressive tradition that currently dominates our schools, “critical thinking” has come to imply a counterpoise to the teaching of “mere facts,”

claims.text: The 'tool conception' of education, which views critical thinking as a formalistic habit independent of specific content, is an incorrect model of real-world cognition.
claims.source_passage: This tool conception, however, is an incorrect model of real-world critical thinking.

claims.text: Independent-mindedness and critical thinking are always predicated on the possession of relevant factual knowledge.
claims.source_passage: Independent-mindedness is always predicated on relevant knowledge: one cannot think critically unless one has a lot of relevant knowledge about the issue at hand.

claims.text: To oppose 'critical thinking' and 'mere facts' is a profound empirical mistake.
claims.source_passage: To oppose “critical thinking” and “mere facts” is a profound empirical mistake.

claims.text: The function of the common school is to enable all citizens to master a shared, school-based culture that allows public-sphere communication.
claims.source_passage: the function of the common school is to enable all citizens to master, in addition to their home cultures, a shared, school-based culture that allows them to communicate and work together in the public sphere.

claims.text: A student's mastery of school-based culture is more difficult if the distance between their home culture and the school culture is great.
claims.source_passage: It is true that because the distance between home and school cultures is great for some students, mastery of the school-based culture is more difficult for them than for other students.

claims.text: Arguments for 'technical bias' in tests are often logically incomplete because they fail to account for differences in cultural preparation for school subjects.
claims.source_passage: The argument is reasonable, but it does not exhaust the logical possibilities, or even the probabilities.

claims.text: Most current standardized tests are free of technical bias as defined by the American Psychological Association.
claims.source_passage: Most current standardized tests are free of technical bias in this sense

claims.text: Blaming unbiased tests for group performance gaps is not a plausible solution to the underlying policy problems of education.
claims.source_passage: Blaming unbiased tests for bias is not a plausible solution.

claims.text: Technical bias in testing is defined as a consistent difference between a group's performance on a test and its performance on a real-world criterion the test measures.
claims.source_passage: As described by the American Psychological Association, technical bias is indicated by a consistent difference between the way a group performs on a test and the way it performs on some real-world criterion that the test is meant to measure.

claims.text: The educational phrase 'developmentally appropriate' lacks scientific meaning and authority.
claims.source_passage: As generally used, the term “developmentally appropriate” is devoid of scientific meaning and lacks scientific authority.

claims.text: The term 'developmentally appropriate' is used to discourage teaching subjects early but never to suggest they are being taught too late.
claims.source_passage: Thus, the term “developmentally appropriate” is generally used to discourage schools from teaching certain subjects too soon, but rarely, if ever, to suggest that subjects are not developmentally appropriate because they are being taught too late.

claims.text: Children's intellectual development involves significant individual variation despite general patterns in processing capacity.
claims.source_passage: But they have also found that there is a great amount of individual variation in children’s intellectual development.

claims.text: After age six, the sequence of school-based learning is determined by prior knowledge and practice rather than biological nature or chronological age.
claims.source_passage: The consensus among psychologists is that after age six or so, school-based learnings follow a sequence determined not principally by nature or by chronological age but mainly by prior knowledge, practice, and experience.

claims.text: Withholding demanding knowledge at an early age based on 'developmental appropriateness' results in differential educational deprivation across social classes.
claims.source_passage: When an elementary school declines to teach demanding knowledge and skills at an early age, the school is unwittingly withholding education differentially from different social classes.

claims.text: The doctrine of developmental appropriateness has had deleterious effects on social justice.
claims.source_passage: As a result, the doctrine of developmental appropriateness, which holds back all students, has had especially deleterious effects on disadvantaged children and on social justice.

claims.text: Knowledge acquired with effort and time is more likely to be retained and integrated than knowledge presented verbally.
claims.source_passage: The premise is true that knowledge acquired on one’s own, with difficulty and by expending lots of time and effort, is more likely to be retained than knowledge presented verbally.

claims.text: Discovery learning is highly inefficient compared to other teaching methods.
claims.source_passage: Second, discovery learning has proved to be very inefficient. Not only do students sometimes fail to gain the knowledge and know-how they are supposed to gain, but they do not gain it very fast.

claims.text: The disparaging term 'drill and kill' falsely implies that practice is unnecessary and that skills are acquired automatically through naturalistic pedagogy.
claims.source_passage: The phrase implies that drill and practice kills the interest and joy children have in learning. At the same time, it implies that needed learnings will automatically be acquired in the ordinary course of schooling by using naturalistic pedagogy...

claims.text: American schools are bizarrely inconsistent in valuing drill and practice for athletics but disparaging it for academics.
claims.source_passage: The factual bases for these claims do not exist, and are invariably contradicted by the attitudes schools take toward pedagogy when it comes to athletics, a bizarre inconsistency in American schools.

claims.text: Drill and practice are as essential to complex intellectual performance as they are to musical performance.
claims.source_passage: They appear to be just as essential to complex and creative intellectual performance as they are to the performance of a virtuoso violinist.

claims.text: The phrase 'drill and kill' implies that repetitive practice destroys a child's interest and joy in learning.
claims.source_passage: The phrase implies that drill and practice kills the interest and joy children have in learning.

claims.text: Progressive rhetoric assumes that necessary knowledge will be acquired automatically through naturalistic pedagogy such as discovery learning or the project method.
claims.source_passage: At the same time, it implies that needed learnings will automatically be acquired in the ordinary course of schooling by using naturalistic pedagogy like “discovery learning,” “thematic learning,” and the “project method”

claims.text: There is no factual basis for the claim that drill and practice is inherently harmful or unnecessary.
claims.source_passage: The factual bases for these claims do not exist, and are invariably contradicted by the attitudes schools take toward pedagogy when it comes to athletics

claims.text: American schools exhibit a bizarre inconsistency by embracing rigorous drill in athletics while rejecting it in academics.
claims.source_passage: The factual bases for these claims do not exist, and are invariably contradicted by the attitudes schools take toward pedagogy when it comes to athletics, a bizarre inconsistency in American schools.

claims.text: Developing basic knowledge and skills to the level of automaticity requires extensive drill and practice.
claims.source_passage: Development of basic knowledge and skills to the level of automatic and errorless performance will require a great deal of drill and practice.

claims.text: Cognitive psychology and neurophysiology support the necessity of distributed practice for skill acquisition.
claims.source_passage: This view is strongly supported by cognitive psychologists and neurophysiologists, who have shown that many skills require repeated experience and “distributed practice” to be learned.

claims.text: The assumption that repeated practice can be successfully replaced by embedding it in naturalistic themes has been discredited.
claims.source_passage: But the assumption that repeated practice can be successfully avoided, or that it can be sufficiently ensured by being embedded in naturalistic themes or projects, has been discredited.

claims.text: Exhibitions (performance-based assessments) are excellent devices for motivating students at the classroom level.
claims.source_passage: Exhibitions are excellent, though subjective, devices for motivating students at the classroom level.

claims.text: In the classroom, effective teaching and learning may sometimes be more important than strict fairness in grading.
claims.source_passage: In the classroom, strict fairness and accuracy in the grading of every student effort, while always to be sought, may sometimes be less important values than effective teaching and learning.

claims.text: The progressivist depiction of 'factory-model schools' as uniformly passive and indoctrinating is a myth.
claims.source_passage: Both pictures are myths. The historical reality is more confusing.

claims.text: The progressive movement itself oversaw the creation of large-scale school systems in the 1920s and '30s.
claims.source_passage: The progressive movement itself presided over the creation of enlarged school systems in the 1920s and ’30s, even as it promoted progressive pedagogical reforms

claims.text: No modern industrial nation has managed to avoid elements of the 'factory model' in educating large populations.
claims.source_passage: No modern industrial nation has been able to avoid some elements of the “factory model” in its efforts simply to educate ever larger percentages of the population.

claims.text: Factory-model hierarchies and traditional pedagogy are not indissolubly linked; it is possible to have progressive classrooms within a hierarchical system.
claims.source_passage: What is really at stake in the polemical use of the term is the association of the factory model with “traditional” pedagogy, as though the two were indissolubly wedded. On the contrary, within a factory-like, hierarchical school system, it is possible to have nontraditional, progressive-style classrooms.

claims.text: Progressive ideas currently dominate the educational hierarchy in the United States.
claims.source_passage: That is precisely the arrangement we have today in the United States. Progressive ideas dominate the system’s hierarchy.

claims.text: The current American school system is ineffective because progressive ideas are educationally ineffective.
claims.source_passage: What makes our current system ineffective is the educational ineffectiveness of those ideas.

claims.text: The best hope for improving the school system is to provide coherent teaching with specific, coherent goals.
claims.source_passage: The best hope for improving our “factory” system... is to provide more coherent and focused teaching, with a view to achieving more specific and coherent goals.

claims.text: Understanding is logically and practically dependent upon a knowledge of the subordinate facts being interrelated.
claims.source_passage: But those interrelations are also facts (if they happen to be true), and their existence also depends entirely upon a knowledge of the subordinate facts that are being interrelated. Since understanding depends on facts, it is simply contradictory to praise understanding and to disparage facts.

claims.text: Interrelations between facts are themselves facts if they are true.
claims.source_passage: But those interrelations are also facts (if they happen to be true), and their existence also depends entirely upon a knowledge of the subordinate facts that are being interrelated.

claims.text: The opposition between facts and understanding is a hallmark of progressive educational ideology.
claims.source_passage: The opposition expressed in this and similar phrases between facts and understanding is a hallmark of progressivism.

claims.text: The proposition that facts are soon outdated has achieved axiomatic status within the American educational community.
claims.source_passage: Phrased in various ways, this is one of the most frequently stated antifact propositions of the American educational community. From being so often repeated, it has achieved axiomatic status.

claims.text: William Heard Kilpatrick was the primary individual responsible for popularizing the 'facts-are-outdated' doctrine in the 1920s.
claims.source_passage: Its ultimate originator may not have been William Heard Kilpatrick, but in the 1920s he was certainly the doctrine’s chief promulgator and popularizer.

claims.text: The constant change in history and technology provides a reason to prioritize teaching stable, central facts that do not change rapidly.
claims.source_passage: But this truism would seem to be a good argument for teaching the central facts (for instance, the elements of the periodic table) which do not change rapidly, if at all, and which are useful for understanding and coping with the changes that do occur.

claims.text: There is no established research case proving that significant factual knowledge is transitory or loses its utility quickly.
claims.source_passage: But a careful case has not yet been made for the transitoriness of significant factual knowledge.

claims.text: Factual knowledge is a prerequisite for the development of higher-order skills and thinking skills.
claims.source_passage: Facts are central to “higher-order skills,” and therefore need to be strongly emphasized even (or especially) when the goal of education is seen to be the development of “understanding” and of “thinking skills.”

claims.text: Multisensory learning is an effective method for fixing and integrating a child's learning.
claims.source_passage: Multisensory learning is indeed an excellent method for integrating and fixing what a child learns, for instance, the use of tactile methods to help children learn the letters of the alphabet.

claims.text: The term 'hands-on' is frequently used as a polemical tool to favor the project method over whole-class instruction.
claims.source_passage: Very often the term “hands-on” is an honorific term used to praise the progressivist “project method” of education and to disparage a “whole-class instruction,” which is conducted mainly by visual and verbal means.

claims.text: Project-based methods such as 'discovery learning' and 'thematic learning' are research-proven to be inefficient and inequitable.
claims.source_passage: The research suggests that such methods are uncertain, unfair (not all children learn from them), and inefficient, and therefore should be used sparingly.

claims.text: Anti-verbal educational prejudices disadvantage students who lack a breadth of verbal learning outside of school.
claims.source_passage: Antiverbal prejudices spell disaster for disadvantaged students, who have not been exposed to a breadth of verbal learning outside the school.

claims.text: The belief that 'higher-order skills' can be taught as content-independent tools is a formalistic misconception rejected by cognitive psychology.
claims.source_passage: Behind this contrast between higher-order thinking skills and lower-order information lies the formalistic tool conception of education, which has been repudiated by mainstream cognitive psychology.

claims.text: Higher-order skills are invariably and necessarily conjoined with relevant, domain-specific information.
claims.source_passage: Higher-order skills are invariably and necessarily conjoined with a great deal of relevant, domain-specific information.

claims.text: The learning of higher-order skills alone does not suffice to produce critical thinkers.
claims.source_passage: If in fact the learning of higher-order skills did suffice to produce critical thinkers prepared for the challenges of the twenty-first century, we and our students would be very fortunate indeed and could forgo a great deal of the hard work associated with gaining factual knowledge

claims.text: It is an unfounded prejudice to assert that strategies for using information are of a higher order than the information itself.
claims.source_passage: It is mere prejudice to assert that the strategies associated with using domain-specific information are of a “higher order” than the knowledge itself.

claims.text: Holistic learning methods are less successful when used to teach specialized subjects like mathematics that require extensive practice.
claims.source_passage: The method is less successful when used to teach a specialized subject or skill like mathematics, which requires a lot of practice.

claims.text: The exclusive use of holistic or naturalistic methods is less effective than using them sparingly within focused, goal-directed pedagogies.
claims.source_passage: The exclusive use of holistic or naturalistic methods has been shown to be less effective than using it sparingly within more focused, goal-directed pedagogies.

claims.text: The failure of holistic teaching is caused by its injudicious overuse based on the false belief that naturalistic methods automatically lead to good results.
claims.source_passage: As with most progressivist methods, it is not the technique itself but its injudicious overuse, in the confidence that naturalistic methods automatically lead to good results, which has made much holistic teaching ineffective.

claims.text: The practical result of modern egalitarian 'individual differences' terminology has been de facto ability tracking.
claims.source_passage: However, because mass education cannot be organized into individual tutorials, the practical result of the current egalitarian terminology has been de facto ability tracking.

claims.text: The term 'individual differences' is used to rationalize expecting and demanding less from disadvantaged students.
claims.source_passage: Too often, the term “individual differences” can become a rationalization for expecting and demanding less from children for whom we need to provide more support—inherently able students from disadvantaged homes.

claims.text: Individualized tutorial instruction is by general agreement the most effective form of schooling.
claims.source_passage: individualized tutorial instruction is, by general agreement, the most effective form of schooling.

claims.text: Attempts to provide individualized instruction in public schools often result in individual neglect for other students in the form of silent seatwork.
claims.source_passage: For that reason, an attempt in the public schools to provide individual instruction to some students often results in individual neglect for others, in the form of isolated, silent seatwork.

claims.text: In typical school settings, the best results for individual students are gained by predominant use of whole-class instruction.
claims.source_passage: In typical schools, the best results for most individual students are gained not by one-on-one tutorials but by a predominant use of whole-class instruction, in which all students participate.

claims.text: Research results on the effectiveness and validity of specific learning styles are decidedly mixed.
claims.source_passage: The results of research on learning styles are decidedly mixed.

claims.text: There is no solid support in literature for the claim that different ethnic groups possess different learning styles.
claims.source_passage: The claims for different styles among different ethnic groups are disputed in the literature.

claims.text: While purely verbal and passive rote learning is inferior to internalized learning, the critique of rote learning has been used as a blunt instrument to attack all factual knowledge.
claims.source_passage: These valid objections to purely verbal, fragmented, and passive education have, however, been used as a blunt instrument to attack all emphasis on factual knowledge and vocabulary.

claims.text: Effective teachers use a diversity of approaches to avoid boring students and to ensure different methods reach different students.
claims.source_passage: Effective teachers have always taught through a diversity of approaches, both in order to avoid boring students through obvious repetition and in the hope that different approaches will stick with different students.

claims.text: The only economically feasible and fair system of schooling is one that employs a generous amount of effective whole-class instruction.
claims.source_passage: Since the only economically feasible and fair system of schooling is one that engages all students in a class most of the time (i.e., a system that employs a generous amount of effective whole-class instruction), one policy implication of different learning styles is that teachers should vary their teaching by using visual aids, concrete examples, and tactile experiences as well as verbal concepts in presenting what is to be learned.

claims.text: Intellectual capital is highly correlated with a person's ability to earn more money and gain more knowledge.
claims.source_passage: Studies have shown that the level of a person’s intellectual capital is highly correlated with a person’s ability to earn still more money and to gain still more knowledge and skill.

claims.text: The accumulation of knowledge and skill follows a compounding principle where existing capital facilitates the acquisition of more capital.
claims.source_passage: As with money capital, the more knowledge and skill one already has, the more one can readily acquire.

claims.text: Teaching metacognition is useful but insufficient because expert skills depend on domain-specific knowledge.
claims.source_passage: But since expert skills are also dependent on domain-specific knowledge, the teaching of metacognition in this narrow sense is recognized as a useful but not sufficient help in learning a skill.

claims.text: Intellectual capital is the primary tool for future learning and earning, not 'inert' baggage.
claims.source_passage: Hence, intellectual capital, repudiated under the tool conception as inert, soon-to-be-outdated baggage, is in reality the main tool of future learning and earning.

claims.text: 'Discovery learning' and 'hands-on learning' are modern adaptations of the older 'learning by doing' formulation.
claims.source_passage: Terms currently preferred to “learning by doing” are “discovery learning,” and “hands-on learning,” but it is important to remember that these latter-day phrases are adaptations of the earlier formulation.

claims.text: The theory that students will reliably discover needed learnings by performing 'holistic' activities has been discredited.
claims.source_passage: By performing “holistic” activities, the student, it is claimed, will reliably discover the needed learnings. This is an attractive doctrine, but it is also a highly theoretical one that has proved to be false.

claims.text: Learning by doing is among the least effective pedagogies available to teachers.
claims.source_passage: Both the recent history of American education and controlled observations have shown that learning by doing and its adaptations are among the least effective pedagogies available to the teacher.

claims.text: The opposition between learning skills and factual knowledge is a misleading opposition with tragic economic and social consequences.
claims.source_passage: As this book explains in some detail, the opposition between learning skills and factual knowledge is an almost totally misleading opposition that has had tragic economic and social consequences.

claims.text: The slogan 'less is more' has encouraged teachers and students to slack off and avoid subjects.
claims.source_passage: the catchiness of the paradoxical formulation should not be permitted to mask the doubtfulness of the idea as a general proposition that can reliably guide teaching or curriculum making... especially one that has all too often encouraged both teachers and students to slack off.

claims.text: In early schooling, breadth of knowledge is preferable to depth.
claims.source_passage: In general, contrary to the motto, breadth is preferable to depth in early schooling, where the child should be provided with a conspectus of the various domains of knowledge and experience

claims.text: Specialized depth should be encouraged in later high school and university once a broad background is secured.
claims.source_passage: In the later grades of high school and at the university, when a student has already secured a broad enough background to enable future learning in many fields, he or she should be encouraged to focus more narrowly and probe more deeply.

claims.text: Learning how to fish requires a great deal of domain-specific knowledge, not just equipment and general techniques.
claims.source_passage: Indeed, even learning how to fish requires a great deal of domain-specific knowledge—not just fishing equipment and a few techniques.

claims.text: The progressive tradition and teachers influenced by it sometimes view skipping subjects as a virtue under the 'less is more' philosophy.
claims.source_passage: If less is more, than skipping a subject altogether might begin to seem a virtue—an attitude not altogether foreign either to the progressive tradition or to many teachers who have been influenced by it.

claims.text: Providing children with a conspectus of knowledge domains allows new learnings to be integrated into their existing web of understanding.
claims.source_passage: where the child should be provided with a conspectus of the various domains of knowledge and experience so that new learnings can be readily integrated into his or her web of understanding and belief.

claims.text: Specialization and deep probing of subjects should be reserved for the later grades of high school and university.
claims.source_passage: In the later grades of high school and at the university, when a student has already secured a broad enough background to enable future learning in many fields, he or she should be encouraged to focus more narrowly and probe more deeply.

claims.text: The balance between depth and breadth requires complex judgment based on subject matter, purpose, and the stage of schooling.
claims.source_passage: In most cases, the balance between depth and breadth is a subject of a complex judgment that takes into account subject matter, the purpose, and the stage of schooling.

claims.text: Schools have a primary duty to ensure all students are competent to learn throughout their lives.
claims.source_passage: Today, when new technologies must be mastered and even new professions learned, the task of making everyone competent to learn throughout life is a primary duty of the schools.

claims.text: The progressive tradition makes an empirical mistake in believing that general competencies like critical thinking do not depend on accumulated knowledge and vocabulary.
claims.source_passage: But the dominant progressive tradition has made a fundamental empirical mistake in believing that these general competencies do not depend upon the accumulation of knowledge and vocabulary

claims.text: Transferable lifelong competencies do not arise naturally from holistic or integrated activities.
claims.source_passage: and in believing that transferable lifelong competencies will arise naturally from “holistic,” integrated activities.

claims.text: Lifelong competencies like reading and critical thinking depend upon domain-specific factual and verbal knowledge.
claims.source_passage: Lifelong competencies, including reading, writing, and critical thinking, depend upon the domain-specific factual and verbal knowledge spurned by many present-day “reformers.”

claims.text: Isolated facts are meaningless; understanding requires connecting facts.
claims.source_passage: Understanding does mean connecting facts; isolated facts are meaningless.

claims.text: Facts are absolutely necessary to understanding.
claims.source_passage: Facts are absolutely necessary to understanding.

claims.text: Whether facts are 'dead' or fragmented depends on the teacher and student, not the facts themselves.
claims.source_passage: Whether they are dead and fragmented depends upon teachers and students, not upon the facts themselves

claims.text: Facts are required for understanding and can be vital and interesting in their own right.
claims.source_passage: which are not only required for understanding but are sometimes immensely vital and interesting in their own right.

claims.text: The term 'metacognitive skills' has an illegitimate, nontechnical meaning that aligns it with the antiknowledge tool conception of education.
claims.source_passage: The illegitimate, broader application of the term identifies it with “accessing skills,” “critical-thinking skills,” “problem-solving skills,” and other expressions of the antiknowledge tool conception of education.

claims.text: In scientific literature, metacognition means a self-conscious awareness of one’s own procedures in performing skilled activities.
claims.source_passage: Technically, in the scientific literature, “metacognition” means a self-conscious awareness of one’s own procedures in performing skilled activities.

claims.text: Expert performance is characterized by the self-conscious monitoring of one's own activities.
claims.source_passage: Such self-conscious monitoring of one’s own activities is characteristic of expert performance.

claims.text: Teaching self-conscious monitoring (metacognition) can accelerate the learning of reading and problem-solving.
claims.source_passage: The teaching of such self-conscious monitoring can speed up the learning of reading and problem-solving skills.

claims.text: Students who learn to set and meet study goals, such as scanning for main meaning, are better able to work independently.
claims.source_passage: Children who have learned how to set and meet such study goals for themselves (e.g., how to scan a text for the main meaning, how to decide on what is more or less important in a subject with respect to their own study aims) are students who are better able to work independently.

claims.text: Study skills and metacognitive monitoring should only be encouraged if they do not displace or distract from solid subject-matter knowledge.
claims.source_passage: Such study skills should clearly be encouraged where this can be done effectively without displacing or distracting from solid subject-matter knowledge.

claims.text: The popularity of multiaged classrooms is likely driven by political and ideological pressures rather than demonstrated effectiveness.
claims.source_passage: The recent popularity of this idea may owe more to political and ideological pressures than to the demonstrated effectiveness of the practice.

claims.text: The academic preparation gap between children of the same age in American schools would be reduced by a more coherent, specific curriculum and grade-by-grade standards.
claims.source_passage: This preparation gap would be reduced by a more coherent and specific curriculum and by more accountability for definite grade-by-grade standards.

claims.text: Multiaged classrooms represent a form of tracking that 'marches under the banner' of 'learning at one’s own pace' to avoid egalitarian backlash.
claims.source_passage: Another cause is the egalitarian reaction against ability tracking, which means that tracking, if it is to exist, has to march under the banner of “learning at one’s own pace.”

claims.text: Multiaged classrooms contain a disproportionate number of older students from disadvantaged homes and ethnic minorities.
claims.source_passage: The most troublesome feature of the multiaged classroom is the disproportionate number of older students in each learning group who come from disadvantaged homes and who belong disproportionately to ethnic minorities.

claims.text: Officially sanctioning the slow progress of disadvantaged students in multiaged classrooms perpetuates social unfairness.
claims.source_passage: The result of officially sanctioning their slow progress is a perpetuation of social unfairness, as detailed in the entry under “At their own pace.”

claims.text: Howard Gardner's specific taxonomy of multiple intelligences and his general interpretation of them are not widely accepted by the psychological community.
claims.source_passage: Neither Gardner’s specific taxonomy nor his general interpretation is widely accepted by the psychological community.

claims.text: Schools lack the competence to classify and rank children based on speculative measures of multiple intelligences.
claims.source_passage: Despite the fact that schools are not competent to classify and rank children on these highly speculative psychological measures, the concept has become highly popular

claims.text: No descriptive theory of multiple intelligences can prescribe what policies and methods schools should pursue.
claims.source_passage: Miller gets to the educational heart of the matter when he observes that even if the classifications were right, no descriptive theory of multiple intelligences could tell us what policies and methods schools ought to pursue.

claims.text: The common-school tradition implies schools must encourage students' strengths while also overcoming their weaknesses in literacy, numeracy, and general knowledge.
claims.source_passage: The common-school tradition of Horace Mann (with which Gardner would probably agree) implies that we should both encourage students’ strengths and overcome their weaknesses, especially in those competencies such as literacy, numeracy, and general knowledge which enable their effective participation in the economic and political life of the nation.

claims.text: Elementary grades require commonality to ensure that each child in a classroom is ready to take the next step in learning.
claims.source_passage: The slogan makes no concession to the practical need for commonality in the elementary grades, which is required simply to ensure that each child in a classroom is ready to take the next step in learning.

claims.text: Stressing a child's individual interests and abilities makes sense in high school once foundations in math, reading, writing, art, and science are established.
claims.source_passage: In high school, on the other hand, once the fundamentals of math, reading, writing, art, and science have been learned, it makes a great deal of sense for the good of the child, as well as of society, to stress a child’s individual interests and abilities.

claims.text: The open classroom, as a form of naturalistic pedagogy, has proved ineffective as a principal technique of schooling.
claims.source_passage: Like all forms of naturalistic pedagogy, the open classroom has proved to be ineffective as a principal technique of schooling.

claims.text: In high school, it is beneficial for both the child and society to emphasize individual interests once fundamentals of core subjects are mastered.
claims.source_passage: In high school, on the other hand, once the fundamentals of math, reading, writing, art, and science have been learned, it makes a great deal of sense for the good of the child, as well as of society, to stress a child’s individual interests and abilities.

claims.text: Outcomes-based education was originally proposed to shift focus from methods like discovery learning toward actual results.
claims.source_passage: In the late 1980s and early ’90s... some professional educators proposed that schools pay relatively less attention to methods of schooling, such as discovery learning, and more attention to results. They labeled this idea “outcomes-based education.”

claims.text: Educational committees drafted outcomes that emphasized democratic attitudes and 'tool metaphors' rather than subject-matter knowledge due to an antipathy toward facts.
claims.source_passage: Because of the general antipathy in the educational community to an emphasis on facts, subject matter, and content, the outcomes drafted by these committees tended not to emphasize knowledge so much as various tool metaphors for education and virtue in the form of democratic attitudes and emotions.

claims.text: Progressivist antipathy to subject-matter knowledge transformed the reasonable idea of outcomes-based education into impractical vagueness.
claims.source_passage: It could also be viewed as the transformation of a reasonable idea into impractical vagueness through progressivist antipathy to subject-matter knowledge.

claims.text: The phrase 'passive listening' is a caricature used to polarize teachers and cause them to reject sensible practices.
claims.source_passage: The caricature is another example of the way a valid point gets carried too far through simplistic slogans, causing teachers to become polarized and to reject sensible practices.

claims.text: Observations of whole-class instruction demonstrate that students are active and learning, rather than passively memorizing facts.
claims.source_passage: But observations of “whole-class instruction” (which see) in the United States and elsewhere provide a very different, far-from-passive picture of what children are actually doing and learning in whole-class instruction.

claims.text: Discovery learning methods may produce less independent-mindedness than traditional methods because they provide less factual knowledge for independent judgment.
claims.source_passage: To the extent that more “active” methods like “discovery learning” provide children with less factual knowledge on which to base independent judgments, the claim to produce independent-mindedness seems doubtful.

claims.text: Performance-based assessments are much less fair and reliable than well-constructed objective tests for high-stakes purposes.
claims.source_passage: The most important criticism is that when used for high-stakes testing, performance tests are much less fair and reliable than well-constructed objective tests.

claims.text: Performance-based assessments do not authentically duplicate or reliably predict real-world performance.
claims.source_passage: Criticisms of performance-based assessment by psychometricians include the observation that “performances” in a school context do not in fact authentically duplicate real-world performance, and do not reliably predict it.

claims.text: Portfolio assessment is virtually useless for large-scale, high-stakes testing.
claims.source_passage: It has proved to be virtually useless for large-scale, high-stakes testing.

claims.text: Portfolio assessment is a version of performance-based assessment where students preserve productions over a semester or year for holistic grading.
claims.source_passage: In portfolio assessment, students preserve in a portfolio all or some of their productions during the course of the semester or year. At the end of the time period, students are graded for the totality of their production.

claims.text: Portfolio assessment is a useful device for the teaching of writing and painting.
claims.source_passage: It is a device that has long been used for the teaching of writing and painting.

claims.text: The nature of a general problem-solving skill has not been scientifically defined.
claims.source_passage: The nature of this general problem-solving skill has not been scientifically defined

claims.text: It is doubtful that a general problem-solving skill actually exists.
claims.source_passage: and it is doubtful that it exists.

claims.text: The ability to solve problems is critically dependent on deep, well-practiced knowledge within a special domain.
claims.source_passage: Work on the problem-solving abilities of specialists like doctors, chess players, and physicists has shown consistently that the ability to solve problems is critically dependent on deep, well-practiced knowledge within the special domain

claims.text: Problem-solving abilities do not readily transfer from one domain to another.
claims.source_passage: and that these problem-solving abilities do not readily transfer from one domain to another.

claims.text: There exists no abstract, generalized, teachable ability to solve problems across a diversity of domains.
claims.source_passage: In short, there seems to exist no abstract, generalized, teachable ability to solve problems in a diversity of domains.

claims.text: Spending school time teaching general skills that do not exist is a waste of resources.
claims.source_passage: For schools to spend time teaching a general skill that does not exist is clearly a waste of resources

claims.text: The focus on general problem-solving illustrates the inherent shortcomings of the tool conception of education.
claims.source_passage: which illustrates the inherent shortcomings of the tool conception of education.

claims.text: The project method was based on a Romantic faith in the superiority of natural learning over artificial learning.
claims.source_passage: The method was based on a Romantic faith in the superiority of a natural to an artificial approach in learning.

claims.text: The project method incorrectly claimed to be based on the latest findings in psychology.
claims.source_passage: It claimed, incorrectly, to be based also on the latest findings in psychology.

claims.text: The project method is the least effective mode of pedagogy used in American schools.
claims.source_passage: Subsequently, observers found the project method to be the least effective mode of pedagogy in use in American schools.

claims.text: The 'project method' persists under modern names like 'discovery learning,' 'hands-on learning,' and 'thematic learning.'
claims.source_passage: But terminology shifted, and the practice itself remained in different forms and under different names, such as “discovery learning,” “hands-on learning,” “holistic learning,” “learning by doing,” and “thematic learning.”

claims.text: Student scores have not significantly risen in schools well-supplied with computers.
claims.source_passage: Some explanation is needed for the fact that student scores have not significantly risen in schools that have been well supplied with computers.

claims.text: Enthusiasm for technology is often based on confidence in technical solutions that lack theoretical explanation or empirical documentation.
claims.source_passage: One fears, however, that the enthusiasm for computers is based upon a confidence in technical solutions that has not been well explained in theory or well documented in experience.

claims.text: The 'tool conception' of education falsely claims education consists of learning tools to enable future learning.
claims.source_passage: the now discredited tool conception of education, which claims falsely that education consists ideally in learning the tools that will enable one to learn things in the future

claims.text: Using computers increases rather than decreases the need for students to have well-practiced habits and mental knowledge.
claims.source_passage: But there is no evidence that its advent has reduced the need for students to have in their minds well-practiced habits and readily available knowledge. Quite the contrary, the more one looks things up via computer, the more often one needs to understand what one is looking up.

claims.text: General 'accessing skills' cannot displace the need for a well-stocked and well-equipped mind.
claims.source_passage: There is no evidence that a well-stocked and well-equipped mind can be displaced by “accessing skills.”

claims.text: The phrase 'research has shown' is often used selectively in education to support claims that are contradicted by more reliable research.
claims.source_passage: Often it is used selectively, even when the preponderant or most reliable research shows no such thing

claims.text: Educational research varies enormously in quality and reliability.
claims.source_passage: Educational research varies enormously in quality and reliability.

claims.text: Small sample sizes and the inability to control significant social, cultural, and personal variables make much educational research insecure.
claims.source_passage: Some research is insecure because its sample sizes tend to be small and a large number of significant variables (social, historical, cultural, and personal) cannot be controlled.

claims.text: Reports of 'successful' educational strategies often assume universal applicability without full documentation.
claims.source_passage: If an article describes a “successful” strategy... the success may not be fully documented, and the idea that the method will work for all students and classrooms is simply assumed.

claims.text: Strict scientific control in educational research is limited by ethical considerations regarding children.
claims.source_passage: There are strong ethical limits on the degree to which research variables can or should be controlled when the subjects of research are children.

claims.text: Greatest confidence in research findings should be placed in refereed journals within mainstream academic disciplines.
claims.source_passage: Greatest confidence can be placed in refereed journals in mainstream disciplines

claims.text: The claim that 'research has shown' a specific method works is often based on selective use of data even when reliable research shows the opposite.
claims.source_passage: Often it is used selectively, even when the preponderant or most reliable research shows no such thing, as in the statement “Research has shown that children learn best with hands-on methods.”

claims.text: Epidemiological research is the most reliable type of research in education.
claims.source_passage: The most reliable type of research in education (as in medicine) tends to be “epidemiological research,” that is, studies of definitely observable effects exhibited by large populations of subjects over considerable periods of time.

claims.text: Educational research that conflicts with well-accepted findings in psychology and sociology should be treated with special skepticism.
claims.source_passage: Educational research that conflicts with such mainstream findings is to be greeted with special skepticism.

claims.text: Some rote learning is indispensable for acquiring vocabulary and arbitrary rules like English spelling.
claims.source_passage: Some purely rote learning is, for example, indispensable to learning the words of one’s own language, since there is rarely a nonarbitrary reason why particular names are attached to particular things in the world.

claims.text: It often doesn't matter how something is learned because the specific episode of learning drops out of memory while the semantic knowledge remains.
claims.source_passage: The episode of learning is insecurely stored in volatile episodic memory; hence, it often doesn’t matter exactly how things are learned, so long as they are learned.

claims.text: The progressive attack on rote learning has disadvantaged children's academic competencies.
claims.source_passage: In the progressive tradition, the attack on rote learning (timely in 1918) has been used to attack factual knowledge and memorization, to the great disadvantage of our children’s academic competencies.

claims.text: Praise in the absence of achievement does not raise student achievement.
claims.source_passage: There is strong evidence in the mainstream literature that praise in the absence of achievement does not raise achievement.

claims.text: Enhancements of self-esteem arise from accurate appraisals of work and realistic encouragement toward actual achievement rather than empty praise.
claims.source_passage: The best enhancements of self-esteem, according to both psychological and process-outcome literature, arise from accurate and matter-of-fact appraisals of a student’s work, as well as realistic encouragement toward effort and actual achievement.

claims.text: Schools are not organized or staffed to reliably secure the spiritual and psychological well-being of students.
claims.source_passage: But schools are not organized, and their staffs are not trained, to reliably secure the spiritual and psychological well-being of students, though good teachers often inspire by example.

claims.text: The primary duty of schools is to teach subject matters and skills effectively.
claims.source_passage: Schools are organized and instituted primarily to teach subject matters and skills, and it is their first duty to do so as effectively as possible.

claims.text: As understanding of a subject becomes deeper, more factual information is required, not less.
claims.source_passage: it is obvious that the phrase “understanding the alphabet” has very different content in different circumstances, and that the “deeper” understanding becomes, the more “mere facts” are required.

claims.text: The phrase 'teaching for understanding' provides very little useful guidance or meaning for educators.
claims.source_passage: it is hard to get much useful guidance or meaning from the phrase “teaching for understanding.”

claims.text: The progressive tradition has used 'holistic' methods to attack the disciplined teaching of reading, writing, and arithmetic.
claims.source_passage: Historically, however, the progressive tradition has continued to attack the disciplined teaching of reading, writing, and arithmetic in favor of “holistic” methods, which supposedly engage and educate the whole child.

claims.text: The disparagement of subject matter has resulted in a diminishment of student competency in those subjects.
claims.source_passage: Not surprisingly, disparagement of “the subject” has resulted in a diminishment of student competency in subject matters.

claims.text: The development of the 'whole child' does not arise automatically from holistic instruction or simulations of real life.
claims.source_passage: Progressivists did not, however, explicitly teach these different spheres of education, but claimed that the development of the whole child would automatically arise from holistic instruction, in which children had to work cooperatively in simulations of real life. In this Romantic faith they were wrong.

claims.text: The inculcation of civic and personal virtue requires guided instruction and monitoring rather than natural development.
claims.source_passage: If we wish to inculcate civic and personal virtue, that too needs to be the object of guided instruction, however indirect and subtle, and monitored for uptake.

claims.text: Modern textbooks are often poor because they are designed to please state textbook-adoption committees rather than to be effective learning tools.
claims.source_passage: many currently available textbooks are unselective and unemphatic, having been designed to pass through textbook-adoption committees in populous states and, therefore, to please everyone.

claims.text: Hands-on, project-style teaching is highly ineffective as a primary alternative to textbook instruction.
claims.source_passage: But the alternative to textbook instruction, in the form of hands-on, project-style teaching, has been shown to be highly ineffective.

claims.text: The most effective subject-matter learning is often achieved through well-written, well-thought-out textbooks.
claims.source_passage: The most effective subject-matter learning is often achieved through the use of well-written, well-thought-out textbooks.

claims.text: Many currently available textbooks are unselective and unemphatic because they are designed to please textbook-adoption committees in populous states.
claims.source_passage: criticisms of textbooks has all too much validity, because many currently available textbooks are unselective and unemphatic, having been designed to pass through textbook-adoption committees in populous states and, therefore, to please everyone.

claims.text: Thematic learning is more successful when used as an occasional device rather than as the primary mode of instruction.
claims.source_passage: As with various forms of the “project method,” however, thematic learning has proved to be more successful when used with prudence as an occasional device than when used consistently as the primary mode of instruction.

claims.text: Mathematics and science require more frequent repetition and practice than history and literature to achieve learning goals.
claims.source_passage: History and literature, for example, generally require fewer reinforcements to achieve a learning goal than do certain aspects of math and science, whose procedures must be often repeated and practiced.

claims.text: Transmission theory is an absolutely necessary principle of civilization and is sound in itself.
claims.source_passage: John Dewey... stated explicitly in Democracy and Education that the transmission theory of education is both sound in itself and an absolutely necessary principle of civilization

claims.text: Effective whole-class instruction is predominantly interactive and involves consistent informal monitoring of student understanding.
claims.source_passage: It is predominantly interactive, with much interchange between students and teacher; it makes frequent use of student performances and student comments on the performances; it involves consistent informal monitoring of the students’ understanding

claims.text: Process-outcome studies show that a predominant use of whole-class instruction is the fairest and most effective organization of schooling.
claims.source_passage: An overwhelming concurrence of reports from process-outcome studies shows that a predominant use of whole-class instruction constitutes the fairest and most effective organization of schooling.

claims.text: Attempting to provide individual tutorial attention in classrooms of twenty to thirty students results in individual neglect.
claims.source_passage: The attempt to sidestep whole-class instruction, and to provide individual tutorial attention in classrooms of twenty to thirty students, results in individual neglect.

claims.text: Many children fail to learn to read under the 'whole-language' instruction method.
claims.source_passage: Some children do learn to read under this method, but many do not.

claims.text: The term 'whole language' has become so vague and colored with non-pedagogical overtones that it should be dropped from use.
claims.source_passage: The term has become so vague, and so colored with non-pedagogical overtones that it could profitably be dropped entirely from use.

claims.text: In fields like science, medicine, and engineering, well-crafted textbooks have always been a necessity.
claims.source_passage: In the sciences and in professions such as medicine and engineering, well-crafted textbooks have always been a necessity.

claims.text: Individual coaching and cooperative learning are most effective when used within a well-organized whole-class context.
claims.source_passage: All these other approaches should be used within a well-organized whole-class context in order to achieve the best and fairest results.

claims.text: Whole-language instruction denotes an approach to reading that emphasizes the joy of literature and avoids drill-like instruction in letter sounds.
claims.source_passage: A phrase denoting an approach to the teaching of reading that emphasizes the joy of good literature and avoids drill-like instruction in letter sounds.

claims.text: In whole-language theory, students are encouraged to learn reading holistically in the same manner they learned their mother tongue.
claims.source_passage: In theory, the method is supposed to motivate children by emphasizing an interest and pleasure in books, and by encouraging students to learn reading holistically, just as they learned their mother tongue

claims.text: Whole-language instruction treats reading as a psycholinguistic guessing game.
claims.source_passage: by encouraging students to learn reading holistically, just as they learned their mother tongue—as a “psycholinguistic guessing game.”

claims.text: Whole language has evolved from a pedagogical method into a philosophy of life and teaching muddled by pseudopolitical associations.
claims.source_passage: “Whole language,” like “outcomes-based education,” has grown and spread far beyond its initial confined meaning to become a philosophy of life and teaching, muddled by pseudopolitical associations.

claims.text: Large-scale implementation of whole-language instruction has yielded unsatisfactory results.
claims.source_passage: After large-scale experience with its unsatisfactory results, especially in California, some former adherents of whole language now advocate a “mixed” approach

claims.text: No well-regarded reading scholar currently advocates for an approach that neglects phonics and phonemic awareness.
claims.source_passage: No well-regarded scholar in the field of reading now advocates an approach that neglects phonics and phonemic awareness.

claims.text: With proper instruction, nearly every child can achieve grade-level reading by the end of second grade.
claims.source_passage: Many experts believe that with proper instruction nearly every child can read at grade level by the end of first or second grade.

claims.text: Educational rhetoric and seductive slogans function to bemuse and bully prospective teachers and the general public into accepting unsound doctrines.
claims.source_passage: Prospective teachers and members of the general public are bemused, bullied, and sometimes infected by seductive rhetorical flourishes like “child-centered schooling” or bullying ones like the dismissive words “drill and kill.”

claims.text: Variations in children's learning rates are driven not only by ability but by differences in energy levels and motivations.
claims.source_passage: It is true that different children learn at different rates because of variations in their abilities, energy levels, and motivations.

claims.text: Primal human instincts, such as the inherent residue of competitiveness, should be moderated and civilized by the educational process.
claims.source_passage: Evolutionary psychologists have argued that all humans retain a residue of competitiveness. Of course, these primal instincts should be moderated and civilized.

claims.text: The distinction between discovery learning and guided instruction is often a false dichotomy because both are fundamentally constructive cognitive processes.
claims.source_passage: But since being told things is also a constructive, nonpassive process... behind the ponderous rhetoric lies the tacit admission that both discovery learning and guided learning are constructed.

claims.text: The shared school-based culture that common schools provide is not static but is a subject for continual democratic negotiation.
claims.source_passage: The changing character of this school-based culture is a continual subject for democratic negotiation.

claims.text: Children typically undergo a significant increase in their cognitive processing capacity between the ages of three and five.
claims.source_passage: Psychologists have found that there is usually a distinct rise in children’s “processing capacity” between age three and age five.

claims.text: The cumulative nature of school learning makes it highly unlikely that children who fall behind in the early grades will ever catch up to their peers.
claims.source_passage: Because of the cumulative character of school learning, educationally delayed children rarely catch up.

claims.text: The selectivity of knowledge is a necessary and important principle in curriculum design at all levels of education.
claims.source_passage: The motto is generally valid in one limited respect: selectivity of knowledge is important at all levels.

claims.text: The teaching of civic and personal virtue cannot be assumed to occur automatically but must be guided and monitored for student uptake.
claims.source_passage: If we wish to inculcate civic and personal virtue, that too needs to be the object of guided instruction, however indirect and subtle, and monitored for uptake.

claims.text: Print publication of an educational claim does not confer reliable scientific or professional authority upon that claim.
claims.source_passage: The moral: Print brings no reliable authority to an educational claim.

claims.text: Real-world information intake, such as listening to broadcasts, is too rapid to allow for the use of accessing skills or dictionary-style lookups.
claims.source_passage: A speaker on the radio or television does not pause for listeners to look up the words they don’t know.

evidence.description: The textbook 'Best Practice' is cited as a representative example of how education schools claim a professional consensus on pedagogical principles.
A list of eight major national educational organizations (NCTM, AAAS, NAEYC, etc.) that supposedly share a doctrinal consensus on pedagogy.
General data showing that external goals and rewards improve achievement compared to self-pacing.
Conclusions from reading specialists that almost all children can reach grade level regardless of 'natural' pace.
International comparison of successful school systems that use grade-level standards for all students.
The success of international educational systems in bringing all children to grade level without slowing high achievers.
The consensus among psychometricians regarding the fairness and accuracy of multiple-choice tests over performance assessments.
Historical failure of the alternative to 'banking theory' (critical thinking skills) to improve the conditions of disadvantaged students.
Findings in cognitive psychology that support the 'intellectual capital' model (knowledge facilitates more knowledge).
Observations comparing student interest in subject-matter teaching versus affectively oriented classrooms.
The findings of evolutionary psychologists regarding the permanence of human competitiveness.
Psychological consensus on the unreliability of eyewitnesses as evidence that memory is a 'construct'.
Parental complaints regarding the discouragement of high-achieving students in cooperative learning settings.
The findings of cognitive psychology regarding the relationship between factual knowledge and the ability to think critically.
The American Psychological Association's definition of 'technical bias' in testing.
Parental complaints regarding capable children being discouraged in cooperative learning environments.
APA definition of technical bias as a measure of validity against real-world criteria.
Psychological findings on a rise in children's 'processing capacity' between ages three and five.
